question.text: WACC (Weighted Average Cost of Capital) is used as the discount rate in:

answer.text: Free Cash Flow to Equity (FCFE) model
Free Cash Flow to Firm (FCFF) model

explanation.text: **The Logic:** WACC discounts FCFF because FCFF represents cash available to ALL capital providers (equity + debt). FCFE uses cost of equity only. **The Trap:** FCFE uses cost of equity (not WACC) since it represents cash to equity holders only. DDM also uses cost of equity. **Cross-Exam Context:** Tested in NISM XV Ch 10. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** WACC = Equity weight x Cost of equity + Debt weight x Cost of debt x (1-tax rate). Lower WACC increases valuations.
